howItWorks.intro: This steel beam load calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Uniform Load Capacity
rules.formula: W = 8 x Fb x Sx / (L x 12)
rules.explanation: Maximum uniform load based on allowable bending stress (Fb) and section modulus (Sx).

rules.name: Deflection (Uniform)
rules.explanation: Maximum deflection at midspan for a uniformly loaded simply-supported beam.

howItWorks.outro: Material prices and building codes vary by region. Get local quotes before purchasing.

faq.q: How do I choose the right steel beam size?
faq.a: Beam selection depends on span length, total load, and deflection limits. For residential floor beams, L/360 deflection limit is standard. Always consult a structural engineer for load-bearing applications — this calculator provides estimates only.

faq.q: What is the difference between uniform and point load?
faq.a: A uniform load is spread evenly across the beam (like a floor). A point load is concentrated at one spot (like a column). Point loads create higher stress at the load point, so the same beam carries less total weight under a point load.
